content:
# John Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_005_page_001.jpg, JHN_005_page_002.jpg

1. After these things there was a feast of the Ioudaioi, and the Yeshua went up to Hierosolyma.

2. And there is in the Hierosolyma by the Sheep [Gate] a pool, the [one] called in Hebrew Bethesda, having five porticoes.

3. In these lay a multitude of the sick — blind, lame, withered.

4. [This verse is not present in the Sinaiticus manuscript.]

5. And a certain man was there, having been thirty-eight years in the sickness of him.

6. The Yeshua, seeing this [one] lying and knowing that he already has much time, says to him, "Do you wish to become well?"

7. The sick [man] answered him, "Lord, I do not have a man that when the water is stirred he may cast me into the pool. But while I am coming, another goes down before me."

8. The Yeshua says to him, "Rise, take up the mat of you and walk."

9. And immediately the man became well, and he took up the mat of him and was walking. And it was [the] Sabbath on that day.

10. Then the Ioudaioi said to the [one] having been healed, "It is [the] Sabbath, and it is not lawful for you to take up the mat of you."

11. He answered them, "The [one] having made me well, that [one] said to me, 'Take up the mat of you and walk.'"

12. They asked him, "Who is the man having said to you, 'Take up and walk'?"

13. But the [one] having been healed did not know who it is, for the Yeshua withdrew, a crowd being in the place.

14. After these things the Yeshua finds him in the temple and said to him, "Behold, you have become well. Sin no longer, lest something worse happen to you."

15. The man went away and told the Ioudaioi that the Yeshua is the [one] having made him well.

16. And because of this the Ioudaioi were persecuting the Yeshua, because he was doing these things on [the] Sabbath.

17. But the Yeshua answered them, "The Father of me works until now, and I work."

18. Because of this therefore the Ioudaioi were seeking all the more to kill him, because not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but also was calling the God [the] own Father of him, making himself equal to the God.

19. Then the Yeshua answered and said to them, "Truly, truly I say to you, the Son is not able to do from himself anything, unless he sees the Father doing [it]; for whatever that [one] does, these [things] also the Son does likewise.

20. For the Father loves the Son and shows to him all things that he himself does, and greater works than these he will show to him, that you may marvel.

21. For just as the Father raises the dead and makes alive, so also the Son makes alive whom he wills.

22. For the Father judges no one, but has given all the judgment to the Son,

23. that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. The [one] not honoring the Son does not honor the Father having sent him.

24. Truly, truly I say to you that the [one] hearing the word of me and believing the [one] having sent me has eternal life, and into judgment does not come, but has passed from the death into the life.

25. Truly, truly I say to you that an hour comes, and now is, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of the God, and the [ones] having heard will live.

26. For just as the Father has life in himself, so also to the Son he gave life to have in himself.

27. And authority he gave to him to do judgment, because [the] Son of Man he is.

28. Do not marvel at this, for an hour comes in which all the [ones] in the tombs will hear the voice of him,

29. and will come out — the [ones] having done the good [things] to [a] resurrection of life, and the [ones] having practiced the worthless [things] to [a] resurrection of judgment.

30. I am not able to do from myself anything. Just as I hear, I judge, and the judgment of me is righteous, because I do not seek the will of me but the will of the [one] having sent me.

31. If I testify concerning myself, the testimony of me is not true.

32. There is another testifying concerning me, and I know that the testimony is true which he testifies concerning me.

33. You have sent to Ioannes, and he has testified to the truth.

34. But I do not receive the testimony from man, but these things I say that you may be saved.

35. That [one] was the lamp burning and shining, and you wished for an hour to rejoice in the light of him.

36. But I have the testimony greater than [that of] Ioannes; for the works that the Father has given to me that I should complete them — the works themselves that I do — testify concerning me that the Father has sent me.

37. And the Father having sent me, that [one] has testified concerning me. Neither the voice of him have you ever heard, nor the form of him have you seen.

38. And the word of him you do not have remaining in you, because the [one] whom that [one] sent, this [one] you do not believe.

39. You search the scriptures, because you think in them to have eternal life, and those are the [ones] testifying concerning me.

40. And you do not wish to come to me that you may have life.

41. Glory from men I do not receive.

42. But I have known you, that the love of the God you do not have in yourselves.

43. I have come in the name of the Father of me, and you do not receive me. If another comes in the own name of him, that [one] you will receive.

44. How are you able to believe, glory from one another receiving, and the glory from the only God you do not seek?

45. Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father. The [one] accusing you is Mouses, in whom you have hoped.

46. For if you believed Mouses, you would believe me, for concerning me that [one] wrote.

47. But if the writings of that [one] you do not believe, how will you believe the words of me?

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — "Bethesda":** Sinaiticus reads Βηθεσδά. Other manuscripts have Bethzatha, Bethsaida, or Belzetha. The name likely means "House of Mercy" (בֵּית חֶסְדָּא) or "House of the Outpouring" in Hebrew/Aramaic.

2. **v.4 — Absent verse:** Sinaiticus omits the entire verse about an angel stirring the water. This verse (explaining why the sick waited by the pool) is absent from the earliest manuscripts and was added later to explain the reference to stirred water in v.7. The omission is one of the clearest examples of scribal interpolation in the NT.

3. **v.17 — "The Father of me works until now, and I work":** ὁ πατήρ μου ἕως ἄρτι ἐργάζεται, κἀγὼ ἐργάζομαι — this is the theological core of the chapter. The Jews understood that God does not cease working on the Sabbath (he sustains creation continuously). Jesus claims the same prerogative — placing himself on the level of God.

4. **v.18 — "making himself equal to God":** ἴσον ἑαυτὸν ποιῶν τῷ θεῷ — the Jewish opponents correctly understand the implication of Jesus' claim. The discourse that follows (vv.19-47) does not retract this claim but expands it.

5. **v.39 — "You search the scriptures":** ἐραυνᾶτε τὰς γραφάς — this could be imperative ("Search the scriptures!") or indicative ("You search the scriptures"). Context favors the indicative — Jesus is describing what they already do, then pointing out the irony: the very scriptures they study point to him.

— EMET, AI Translator
